---
title: "Exit Plan"
format: html
---

# Exit Plan

This worksheet helps you define *clear exit thresholds* and *automatic actions* so you can leave quickly without overthinking in a high-risk moment.

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/exit.jpg?raw=true){fig-align="center"}

## 1. My Personal Warning Scale

Define your early warning system:

| Level | What I Feel/Notice | What It Means     |
|-------|--------------------|-------------------|
| 1–3   |                    | Calm / Safe       |
| 4–6   |                    | Caution           |
| 7–8   |                    | High Risk         |
| 9–10  |                    | Leave Immediately |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 2. My Exit Threshold

I will leave when I reach:

-   [ ] Level 5\
-   [ ] Level 6\
-   [ ] Level 7\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_

**Why this level:** \>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 3. Non-Negotiable Rule

Complete this sentence:

> “If I notice \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_, I will leave immediately.”

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 4. 3-Step Exit Script (Automatic Action)

When I hit my threshold:

### Step 1 — Internal Decision (no debate)

> I remind myself:

### Step 2 — Quick Exit Behavior

Choose your default action:

-   [ ] Stand up and gather belongings\
-   [ ] Walk directly to exit\
-   [ ] Text ride / call ride\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Step 3 — Minimal Explanation

Pick ONE simple phrase you will always use:

-   [ ] “I’ve got to head out.”\
-   [ ] “Early morning tomorrow.”\
-   [ ] “Not feeling great — I’m leaving.”\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 5. Decision Traps to Avoid

Check any that apply to you:

-   [ ] “I’ll stay 5 more minutes”\
-   [ ] “I don’t want to be rude”\
-   [ ] “I can handle it”\
-   [ ] Waiting for a ‘perfect’ moment\
-   [ ] Other: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

::: callout
## Exit Rehearsal

Visualize the moment:

-   Where are you standing?\
-   What triggers the decision?\
-   What do you physically do next?\
:::
